methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $2M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Orbital Composites is a Seed-stage company in Robotics & Manufacturing founded by Cole Nielsen, having raised $6.6M to date. The broader sector includes nine peer companies (one public, eight private), though comparable valuations at the same stage and tier proved insufficient for robust peer benchmarking.

Valuation analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, the only applicable triangulation approach given data constraints. Using total raised ($6.6M) as a proxy for current valuation and applying a typical 2.5x Seed-to-next-stage multiplier yields an implied valuation of $6M. A peer-median approach was not feasible: while 11 same-sector companies exist, none met the threshold of three or more comparable firms at the same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier. Relying on all-sector medians would introduce unacceptable noise.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $5M to $7M, centered at $6M. This estimate carries material uncertainty. The single methodological input depends on total capital raised as a proxy—appropriate for early-stage companies but sensitive to capital efficiency and burn patterns not captured here. The absence of tight-fit peer comparables limits confidence in stage-progression multiplier selection. Key unknowns affecting value include product-market traction, technical differentiation within manufacturing robotics, and runway sustainability relative to capital deployed.
